description:
Advances: specled.compiler_context, specled.mix_tasks

Deliverable:
Per refined-plan D6/D7 and specled.decision.tracer_manifest_merge_on_flush:
- lib/specled_ex/compiler/context.ex: FIX Context.load/1 default manifest path — currently Path.join(compile_path, ".mix/compile.elixir") resolves UNDER ebin/ and loads 0 modules (empirically verified; correct sibling path loads 79). New default: Path.join([Path.dirname(compile_path), ".mix", "compile.elixir"]) — dirname-based so an explicit compile_path: override still finds its sibling .mix/. ADD Context.from_mix_project/1: derives app/env/compile_path/manifest_path/build_path from Mix.Project + Mix.env, accepts keyword overrides, delegates to load/1; the ONLY Context function consulting Mix globals (load/1 stays Mix-free). Moduledoc: name it the production entry-point constructor.
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex: build context via @doc false default_context(root) — returns Context.from_mix_project() when Path.expand(root) == File.cwd!(), else nil (Mix.Project describes cwd's project, not an arbitrary root; keeps tmp-root task tests behaviorally unchanged). Pass into SpecLedEx.branch_check(index, root, base: opts[:base], context: context). BranchCheck and Orchestrator need ZERO changes (the :context opt path already exists end-to-end).
- Specs in this stage: compiler_context.spec.md — ADD from_mix_project (must: only Mix-consulting constructor), ADD default_manifest_path (must: default resolves to sibling .mix/compile.elixir of the app dir; integration canary asserts NON-EMPTY manifest through default derivation), REFINE manifest_fixture_integration statement to require the non-empty assertion (id/priority preserved); mix_tasks.spec.md — ADD check_builds_compile_context (must: spec.check constructs Context via from_mix_project when root==cwd, threads into branch guard; --root elsewhere passes no context). Scenarios + tagged_tests verification covers for all, execute: true.
- CHANGELOG.md (Unreleased): merge-on-flush + ghost filtering + context wiring; explicit consumer note: implementation-tier closures now walk the full in-project graph so committed implementation hashes CHANGE — consumers must re-seed (delete implementation section of .spec/realization_hashes.json, run mix spec.check once on a clean tree; Atlas: re-enable tier in .spec/config.yml first). specled_ex's own baseline is api_boundary-only and unaffected.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: mix test test/specled_ex/compiler/manifest_test.exs test/specled_ex/compiler/manifest_integration_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_check_test.exs; full mix test; mix spec.check
- Behaviors to demonstrate: default-path unit test (tmp tree with fixture-shaped .mix/compile.elixir); from_mix_project in-process (Mix.Project is spec_led_ex in test VM: non-empty manifest containing a known SpecLedEx.* key, compile_path exists); manifest_integration_test STRENGTHENED to map_size(context.manifest) > 0 and Manifest.sources_for(manifest, Sample) != [] via DEFAULT derivation (the assertion that would have caught the ebin/ bug — is_map() alone let an empty map pass); spec_check default_context: %Context{} with non-empty manifest for File.cwd!(), nil for tmp root. Final acceptance run in worktree: mix compile (warm) -> mix spec.check -> mix spec.check again — both green, identical findings (exercises S1+S2+S3 together). New @tag spec: annotations required.

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/compiler/tracer.ex, lib/specled_ex/compiler/tracer/** (S1)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/** (S2)
- .spec/specs/compiler_tracer.spec.md, .spec/specs/implementation_tier.spec.md
- mix.exs, .spec/config.yml
Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- lib/specled_ex/compiler/context.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex
- .spec/specs/compiler_context.spec.md
- .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md
- test/specled_ex/compiler/manifest_test.exs
- test/specled_ex/compiler/manifest_integration_test.exs
- test/mix/tasks/spec_check_test.exs
- CHANGELOG.md

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do NOT enable the implementation tier in specled_ex's own .spec/config.yml (consumer-side acceptance, D9c).
- No behavior change for --root <elsewhere> invocations (context stays nil).
- load/1 must remain Mix-free (preserves specled.compiler_context.load_from_opts and mechanical greps in manifest_test.exs).
- spec.check.ex touch impacts api_boundary/branch_guard/mix_tasks surfaces: mix_tasks.spec.md co-changes here; the other two should attest clean via their Mix.Tasks.Spec.Check.run/1 bindings. If a gate run still flags them, co-change those spec files minimally in this stage — do NOT use a severity trailer (trailers go inert once merged to main).

Merge gate:
- [ ] mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- [ ] mix test
- [ ] mix format --check-formatted && mix deps.unlock --check-unused
- [ ] mix spec.check
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: a7c01b31-1832-4731-b697-52202d55f59c
Log: ~/.claude/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/a7c01b31-1832-4731-b697-52202d55f59c.jsonl

Commit: 7b73e0d (base: S2's 4538bd1)
Files touched: lib/specled_ex/compiler/context.ex, lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex, .spec/specs/compiler_context.spec.md, .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md, test/specled_ex/compiler/manifest_test.exs, test/specled_ex/compiler/manifest_integration_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_check_test.exs, CHANGELOG.md, .spec/state.json (regen)
Tests added: default-manifest-path unit tests (real manifest copied into a tmp app-dir tree; explicit compile_path override finds its own sibling .mix/), from_mix_project in-process tests (non-empty manifest containing SpecLedEx.Parser, compile_path exists, keyword override wins), manifest_integration strengthened to map_size > 0 + sources_for(Sample) != [] via the DEFAULT derivation (the assertion that would have caught the ebin/ bug), default_context/1 tests (cwd -> %Context{} with non-empty manifest; relative-dot root -> context; tmp root -> nil)
Verification stubs flipped: new tagged_tests blocks (compiler_context: default_manifest_path + from_mix_project; mix_tasks: check_builds_compile_context) added at execute: true

Deviations from ticket text, with rationale:
1. mix_tasks.spec.md got requirement + verification cover but NO scenario — that spec file has no spec-scenarios section at all (~20 must requirements, none with scenarios, validates green); following the file's established structure instead of singling out one requirement.
2. Requirement id is specled.tasks.check_builds_compile_context (the file's ids use the specled.tasks.* prefix, not specled.mix_tasks.*).

Risk check that mattered: wiring a context changes Binding.resolve's resolution path for ALL tiers; verified api_boundary committed hashes are stable through the context path — spec.check green with zero drift findings.

Acceptance run: warm compile then consecutive mix spec.check runs — three consecutive green with byte-identical findings output (5 info-level file-touch findings, two attested clean by the realization machinery itself). One earlier acceptance attempt had a one-off merged-run verification_command_failed, green on all re-runs, output lost to a narrow grep; logged with the day's other one-off in tracking ticket specled_-f98.

Gates: mix compile --warnings-as-errors PASS; mix test 888 tests 3 properties 0 failures; mix format --check-formatted PASS; mix deps.unlock --check-unused PASS; mix spec.check status=pass errors=0.

Summary: mix spec.check now hands the realization tiers a real compile context (Context.from_mix_project/1, root==cwd guarded), fixing the production nil-manifest gap; Context.load/1's default manifest path bug (ebin/.mix, 0 modules) is fixed with the canary strengthened so it cannot silently return; CHANGELOG carries the consumer re-seed note for implementation-tier baselines.

title: S3: Production Context wiring + Context.load manifest-path fix + release note
